title: Product: Active Pipeline Review
summary: This product meeting reviewed the active pipeline, focusing on Epilito Auto Group engagement, transcription and optimization fixes, and CRM integrations. Key updates included a backup system for missing transcripts, VCON database optimization, and live production-level redaction for Frontline. Discussions covered e-lead improvements, API failure rates, and VIN Solutions call note hyperlinks. Action items were assigned for API failure investigation, VIN Solutions testing, system access review, and onboarding flow development.

decisions.description: Implemented a backup system for missing transcripts using Crescendo, OpenAI, and Deepgram.
Optimized the VCON database to reduce timeouts and issues in GPT, search, signals, and reports.
Removed the 25-signal limit per user for Straw users by optimizing performance.
Fixed a real-time signals bug by adjusting prompts and switching to a more cost-effective GPT model.
Added Spanish voicemail detection and improved voicemail delivery reliability.
Implemented automatic detection of bad phone numbers based on carrier messages.
Moved the portal link in VIN Solutions call notes from bottom to top for clickability.
Decided to create a bot to scrape OEM call notes directly from Dealer Socket CRM.
Implemented live production-level redaction for Frontline to share anonymized customer data.
Started a new process for the engineering team to review high-priority IT escalations.

actionItems.task: Investigate the reported 30% API failure rate for leads not pushing.
Test the VIN Solutions API push notes functionality in one store.
Continue working with Holly to obtain the list of users for system access review.
Work on developing a proper onboarding flow for new portal users.
Follow up on the capability to push call notes to Drive Centric CRM.
Kick off discovery and development for the bot to scrape OEM call notes from Dealer Socket.
Bump the portal onboarding flow to a higher priority list.
Begin exploring automation for notes on Techon and Dealer Socket.
Prioritize and implement smaller security fixes, such as removing keys from logs.

tensions: Concerns raised by Vinnie Micciche and Shawna Behen regarding pushing call notes to Drive Centric CRM due to perceived API limitations.
Shawna Behen expressed doubt about the reported 30% API failure rate for leads, stating her team would have noticed.
Challenges noted with Frontline users adapting to AI LLM tools, requiring training on prompt writing.
Thomas Howe highlighted that redaction tagging requires non-repeatable professional services specific to each implementation.
referencesToPast: Previous engagement with Epilito Auto Group and their past interest in a hybrid model for outbound service.
Matt Watson affirmed confidence in an existing API for Drive Centric, though noting past issues with call notes refusal.
Shawna Behen mentioned a bot for Drive Centric that 'we shut off when we're working'.
Shawna Behen explained that Sergey Lyapustin had addressed a hyperlink issue with VIN Solutions from 'last week or the week before'.
Matt Watson mentioned a BMW theme requirement for Straw Chat that he 'did years ago'.
Sergey Lyapustin noted that merging issues based on silent status is 'something we've never been able to fix'.

contributions.description: Reported numerous fixes for transcription and optimization, including a backup system for missing transcripts.
Provided updates on VCON database optimization to reduce timeouts and issues in GPT, search, signals, and reports.
Affirmed confidence in an existing API for Drive Centric CRM, despite past issues with call notes refusal.
Detailed updates to the portal and apps, including removing the 25-signal limit and fixing a real-time signals bug.
Highlighted Spanish voicemail detection, improved voicemail delivery, and automatic bad phone number detection.
Discussed ongoing efforts to resolve e-lead issues, including automated solutions for misleads and duplicates.
Addressed a VIN Solutions hyperlink issue by moving the portal link to the top of the note for clickability.
Announced efforts to create a bot to scrape OEM call notes directly from Dealer Socket CRM.
Noted that an onboarding flow for new portal users is in the backlog, as call notes are a top source.
Confirmed a significant effort is underway to get transcripts for click-to-call issues in VIN Solutions.
Provided an update on security efforts, prioritizing smaller fixes and reviewing system access with Holly.
Highlighted the implementation of live production-level redaction for Frontline to share anonymized data.
Added automated call evaluations and upset customer detection as valuable features for Strawa BDC.
Inquired about customized features for BPO focus and the potential rebranding of Stralled as a BPO.
